# Commandeck

> Commandeck is a cross-platform desktop application (Linux, macOS, Windows) that turns
> shell and SSH commands into a grid of clickable buttons. Click a button and the command
> runs — locally or on a remote machine over SSH — with the output shown in a window.
> It is built for people who want to avoid retyping the same commands, from beginners who
> want to skip the terminal to sysadmins managing several machines. Configuration is plain
> local TOML; there is no cloud, no account, and no telemetry.

## What it is
- A visual command launcher / button-grid dashboard for running shell and SSH commands.
- Native desktop app (PySide6/Qt), not a web panel and not a hosted service.
- Open-core: the free core is licensed AGPLv3; a paid Pro tier adds remote/SSH and power features.

## Key facts
- Platforms: Linux (AppImage), macOS (.dmg), Windows (.exe).
- Free tier (AGPLv3): unlimited local command buttons, categories, icons, colors, default buttons.
- Pro tier ($29/year, 14-day free trial, no credit card): SSH machines, multi-machine buttons,
  multi-select group actions, custom themes, config backup/restore, execution profiles,
  and an MCP server that lets an AI assistant (cloud or a local model) create and run buttons.
- Privacy: no cloud, no account, no telemetry; config is plain-text TOML stored locally.
- The MCP / AI execution is off by default and gated behind three explicit opt-ins and an audit log.

## Typical use cases
- Beginners running everyday commands (disk usage, updates, reboot) without the terminal.
- Sysadmins / homelab users running commands across several machines over SSH in one click.
- Driving the app from a local or cloud AI assistant via the MCP server.

## What makes it different
- It is a desktop app, not a web panel you host: no server, no container, no exposed port, no
  YAML config — you install it and click. SSH is built in (one button can run on several machines).
- Software-only with unlimited on-screen buttons, running full shell/SSH commands (not keystrokes
  like a hardware keypad).
- A visual, discoverable, editable grid instead of memorized aliases or scripts.
- Local and private by design: plain-text config, no cloud, no account, no telemetry.

## Links
- Website and documentation: https://commandeck.app
- Source code (free AGPLv3 core): https://github.com/neurocontrarian/commandeck
- Download: https://github.com/neurocontrarian/commandeck/releases/latest
